reviews.review_description:
Port of an old PC game, but thoroughly enjoyable. Unfortunate name, but that's ok.

Well done Devs. Waiting for DO2 or add on levels.

It's like Dungeon Keeper 2, without the charm, graphics, features.
Sadly it is hard to find anything that is done better than in the original.
I was surprised to see that VR is mostly a hindrance to the gameplay, spells especially. Even though the devs did a good job porting them.
Me (and probably many others) would have thought that DK2 in VR would be great, now I know its not.

But hey at least it exists!

This is a very good game, the replayability is higher then a lot of vr games. But, like the title says, let me say something. This game can be very buggy and not fun at times. Like when I got introduced to the reaper summoner (Cant remember what its called lol) I sold it cause I didn't think it would matter, but oh boy it did because I SPENT THE NEXT HOUR BEATING THE LEVEL JUST TO NOT BE LET PASS! The worst part? You can't buy it again and it doesn't give you a warning or heads up! Another thing, the level Dark Encounter, (I think) can be done before the first wave arrives and before you buy anything! Just place a imp down at a planetarium then quickly pull it away once an enemy notices, boom you win. So overall, this game is great but it could 100% use a few quailty of life changes. Its a game were you have to make it fun for yourself and not try to find the best way to win and to just have fun. (My god that took a while)

developer_response.body:
Sorry for the inconvenience I completely overlooked the fact that a player could sell the ritual room in the Reaper level. This has never been reported to me since the game launch. I will push a fix for that as soon as possible.

Don't hesitate to reach me on our discord if you have other bugs to report. 
https://discord.myron.games

reviews.review_description: This is a great homage to Dungeon Keeper, and feels nostalgic to play. Zooming in, the character and environment design are really fun, and I absolutely love the imps. If you're looking to scratch that itch from the 90's that reminds you of your childhood, this game is completely worth it. Apparently I was so quiet for a few hours, I may have left the house, and my partner came looking for me. I've seen a couple of grumbles about not having the additional features people expected 30 years later, but this is a smaller studio with less resources, and they've done an amazing job. The graphics and interface are a massive step up, in my opinion. This game is awesome.

I played Dungeon Keeper back in the day, liked it well enough to play the second one as well. Maybe that was a long time ago, but quite frankly, I can't remember it being this much fun. The immersion in this is making me feel like some kind of cartoon bad guy, mwahaha. Pick minions up and throw them at the enemy, throw spells and join the fight yourself. I haven't been all the way through yet so can't wait to see what's on the next level. Good job devs, awesome work.

It's an excellent, super entertaining strategy game. It's incredible to be the overlord of your own dungeon. It's challenging, and even though the difficulty level is impossible, I'd like them to increase the difficulty even more, either with more waves of demolitionists or an infinite mode or something that would make it more challenging. I hope they continue to update the game in the future. It's a marvel of virtual reality, and everyone should try it. 

One more thing: I have a problem with an achievement. 

I've played the entire game, all  levels on impossible difficulty, and completed all the speedrun achievements, but I've noticed there's a bug with the Keep the Gold achievement, as it's impossible to obtain. It's a bug  or something, and I'd really like to have the game 100% complete. I hope you can fix it :3
Super easy to pickup and very addictive. Always looking forward to the next unlock to see how my strategies and setups will change. Very much enjoying it as a more relaxing time.

i know this isnt really a tip but I think you should add a endless mode where peaple can make dungeon forever and ever.
great game its fun and ive been looking for a dungeon maker for a while. I do crash a bit when trying to get into the game.
Please Add enLess mode to this fantastic game and sandbox will make it so much more magnificent

It’s enjoyable and has me hooked the whole time. The difficulty scaling is nice and steady and you figure things out as you go pretty easily. 

I have many more levels to go and I look forward to it. This developer continues to make good games and I hope they add some co-op features either to this game or when they make future games!

Overall I recommend playing the game, especially if you enjoyed Deisim; The controls are the same so it’s easy to understand. The graphics are really nice too and I like the dungeon element.
works lovely in VR. Less elaborate then DK but captures the charm. Could use some more challenge too. But highly recommend as DK fan!

bad game does not load crashes headset
AND boring

developer_response.body:
This is the first time crash issues are reported. Don't hesitate to contact me at support@myrongames.com so I can try to help you to fix that.

We made the choice of keeping the first levels simple to allow all players to learn the basics during the first levels but the difficulty quickly increase after a few levels.

reviews.review_description: This game is really fun! I haven't beaten the whole thing yet. But it's been a blast. Really wish I could send troops to the overworld. Seems a little lacking in content. Although it plays just like you think it would. Can't wait to see what yall can do with this game. Loved the other god sim game by the company as well. Keep up the good work! Show them some love ❤️

reviews.review_title: Great, but basic and lacks spirit
reviews.review_description:
Well done to the developers, you have done an amazing job and this is a good game and you have stayed true to the basic mechanics of Dungeon keeper. I'm glad I bought it. That said - it could be SO much better and more replayable. 
It's 2025. If Dungeon keeper had continued to exist, it would be 30 years old and would have evolved into something way better and more complex, with more strategy and replayability - just like the elder scrolls, civilisation and grand theft auto, doom and other games of its time. Underworld overseer lacks features that even the first game has, like progressive creature levelling, creatures learning new skills, creatures having names so they don't all feel like identical chess pieces I couldn't care less about. 
Here are some changes that would make the game better:
-micromanage and upgrage each room - Yes, have the training room. But be able to add specific equipment to it that allows creatures to get better at certain skills -> For example, something to make wizards level up faster such as an obelisk which absorbs their spells.
-Levelling-> I'd love to see creatures be able to level to 10 again - that way, i'd be more selective of who I send to battle. If my level 10 troll dies, it's a bigger loss than 4 random trolls dying who I know will simply come back out of a portal sooner or later. 
-Scavenging-> Some room upgrades can't simply be bought with gold - you need to scavenge things from humans 
-Bring back Misteresses and torture chambers, and converting enemies. 
-Squads -> Instead of all creatures rallying to a flag, organise your own squads with unique rallying flags. 
-Enemies with Personality -> some cocky voice acting when a "champion" arrives to "destroy me" would be great. 
-diverse creatures within the same class - Think training each mage to specialise in e.g. fire, telekenesis, ice; each troll in clubs, boulder throwing, jugganaught. 
I have way more. Please take this as constructive criticism, your game is great.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback. We had to simplify many things to make the game faster because waiting in VR is very annoying but it is true that the game has still ways to be improved. 
We have plans for that if the game find it's audience. Strategy games are not a very popular genre in VR yet unfortunately and as a very small team we don't have the budget of Bullfrog/Electronic Arts.

reviews.review_title: Fun game to keep genre going
reviews.review_description: Well done game for what it is. Glad to see it’s still being updated and hope it continues to be more complex (as stated in previous reviews) within quest parameters.  It’s fun regardless and within a good limit of challenging for casual and harder playing. Anyone who enjoyed dungeon keeper type games should enjoy this too.  Don’t expect it to be 1:1 old dungeon keeper, it’s just similar.

As someone who loved Dungeon Keeper back in the 90's, I'm saddened at how boring / similar most game mechanics are these days.   It's great to see this genre done again in VR, and well.  Its beautiful, works well, a great use for VR.   More people should play this, shocked it doesn't have more reviews.

I just have to share my enthusiasm for the incredible Underworld Overseer—and no, I’m not getting paid for this! 😆

Underworld Overseer is a truly unique VR game, serving as the spiritual successor to the legendary PC classic Dungeon Keeper. That means one thing: You’re the bad guy! Your mission? Build a devious dungeon and defend it against so-called "heroic" intruders. Let’s be honest—we all know that being evil is way more fun.

To attract monstrous allies, you need to design your dungeon wisely, catering to their preferences. Cultists, for instance, demand a library, while trolls are happiest near furnaces. But don’t forget—your minions have needs! They require food, shelter, and, of course, a generous salary (honestly, their wage expectations are outrageous). If they’re unhappy, they might even abandon your dungeon altogether.

While your monstrous companions will help fend off intruders, you can’t control them directly. That’s why a well-thought-out dungeon layout is crucial. Want to make life miserable for those pesky heroes? Set up deadly traps and unleash a range of powerful spells to turn the tide in your favor.

The developers have done an outstanding job capturing the charm of Dungeon Keeper and bringing it to the Quest. The game’s comic-style cel-shaded graphics look fantastic, and the missions are packed with dark humor. Each mission takes around 30 minutes, striking the perfect balance between challenge and accessibility.

For me, Underworld Overseer is one of the best strategy games on the Quest and absolutely deserves more recognition—which is exactly why I’m writing this! If you love strategy games or have always dreamed of running your own dungeon, this is your chance.

It’s an absolute steal for the price and, without a doubt, one of the top strategy games on the Quest. I’ve completed it myself, so trust me when I say:

🔥 A solid 5-star rating! 🔥

reviews.review_title: Great game, really a lot of fun
reviews.review_description:
I was super excited about this game once I heard about it. Sort of like the old dungeon keeper(first game I bought with my own money) but not quite. Let me  start with the bad. It feels limited in what you are able to do. Spells are ok but not amazing, the units are interesting but again limited. You also can't inhabit your minions and the rooms are just ok(where the chickens at?}

However you can play this game for hours and none of it bothers you. It's really fun even if it's not quite dungeon keeper VR. The levels go by quick and you almost always feel like you have something you need to do. Bigger rooms, more money, more units, kill the enemies... It's awesome. If you like strategy games this is almost certainly one of the best on the quest store.
I love this game so much, its dungeon keeper in VR!! I just wish it had the sandbox endless mode of dungeon keeper, then it would be perfect!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words. We are considering adding that sandbox mode if the game find it's audience.

reviews.review_title: Love this game and beat it.
reviews.review_description:
I love this game its very relaxing and you can lose your self for hours in it. 
I didn't know new maps where suppose to be coming . i have no idea if those have dropped yet. but the levels and game i played before learning that today is still 5 stars. if any new levels are coming i look forward to it.

Edit love the new island nice 👍 can't wait to see what's coming next. 



A Mario Maker map style system would give this game infinite play time as well.

I loved every single mechanic in the game all the art styles and the plot of the game my only hope is in any future update. I hope they bring sandbox like in their game Deisim now that would be awesome to see. Ps love your games 
  ￼
I found the game very addictive because of the satisfaction of simply playing the game. The only thing I think they should add is a sandbox mode

This is an absolute treat for fans of dungeon management and dark fantasy. It puts you right in the role of an evil overlord, building and commanding your own monstrous lair—The mechanics are surprisingly deep: you’re recruiting minions, laying traps, managing resources, and defending against pesky heroes who think they can cleanse your domain. Everything from dragging rooms into place to flicking enemies away with a wave of your hand feels powerful and intuitive.
The art style strikes a great balance between grim and quirky, and there’s a nice layer of humor under all the villainy. It's like being inside a living board game where you call the shots—and the carnage.
If you’re into games like Dungeon Keeper  then this is a must-play. In VR, it takes on a whole new level of fun and strategy. Dark and incredibly fun—One to get!!

The game is really good I love the game but there is one thing you should add. You should add sandbox mode when you finish you can be able to play another. Also there should be different maps for the sandbox.
I'm so happy that one of my favorite classic games was reimagined and refreshed for VR.  It's a homage to Dungeon Keeper, but there's new and fresh changes, so you're playing a whole new game.
i would rate it 6 stars.

reviews.review_title: Tell a friend, would well recommend
reviews.review_description:
Normally don't play strategy games but I really enjoyed this one..
Definitely worth playing & putting some time in to..

There was Deisim. An excellent 10/10 God Game with Sandbox pieces. Now, we have this. 12/10 God Game with Sandbox pieces. As Sid Rambo said, I totally agree. Thank you, Myron. You kicked some butt with these games!

Where is the promised end of January update?

Cant wait to play more maps!

developer_response.body: We are a bit late but the update is almost ready. Stay tuned for more maps and new enemies that will force you to change your plans

reviews.review_title: One of the best Dungeon crawlers.
reviews.review_description:
If you are a fan of this type of game this is an essential buy to add to your collection, I’m no fan of these games but I had a really good time.
The passion from the devs is there to see.

Only a few dungeons in, but I’m having a great time so far. If you’ve never played dungeon keeper, or a game like it, this is a great entry point into the genre.

I got this game during a sale and I actually found myself wanting to play it more than some of the more expensive games I also got. It’s a nice relaxing change of pace from other more hectic games, and after a long day at work that’s exactly what I’m looking for. It may not be everyone’s cup of tea, but this genre has always been kinda niche. It’s a great addition to your library if you’re looking for something different!

Feedback: Game still seems new so I’m hoping the devs will put out more updates. Like others have said an endless or sandbox mode would be fun. Even a multiplayer mode where someone could play as humans and spawn enemies might be neat. Or just having another dungeon keeper in a coop mode to help take on a bigger map. 

Maybe some themes for the background so you aren’t just sitting in darkness. Different skins for your minions, hands, dungeon tiles, dungeon core, etc that you can unlock through the campaign. Perhaps talent points like more hp for minions/ faster building/ more starting goblins/ etc. It would just be nice to have some kind of progression and customization to the game.

This game is so chilled and relaxing until you notice that you have been having so much fun building your dungeon that you forget to keep an eye out for those pesky adventurers who want to steal your stuff.

One minute chilled, the next minute blind panic as you realise your defences have been breached.

10/10 fun sit down game!

More games like this needed in vr - vr is great for ALL types of games, not everything has to be sword waving or shootie shootie… ;-)
Fun and original idea in vr.

Love this game it is so awesome. I have high hopes that the devs will make more content for this. In my opinion, this is the best recreation put in vr for our enjoyment. Amazing job devs keep up the good work!! I would give those 5 stars, but it needs a sandbox mode so you can build your own dungeon or free play mode. Would love to see an endless waves mode. Is there any way to make it so you can pick up gold? How about a window that shows the total of every kind of creature you have and the ability to pick them up from this window but be able to pick all of them up if you want so you can drop them all where you want them? Even  if my suggestions are an add-on id, I pay to see those modes.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! Stay tuned we are currently working on a more open level with infinites waves to allow you to create your dungeon with even more freedom and for as long as you want

reviews.review_description: What a great game fully immersive, the mechanics are relatively easy to pick up, I found the depth of strategy involved in managing your domain can be quite challenging, but not impossible. for me there was quite a steep learning curve. Highly recommended for those looking for something more than your average VR title.

reviews.review_title: Awesome game, some ideas for it.
reviews.review_description: Would be kinda nice if there was a sandbox mode. Also I think Golems should be able to actually attack humans and only explode when they die or get thrown hard enough.

What an awesome game!!!
Based on one of my most favourite classic games ever… Dungeon Keeper 😈 

Is it a faithful recreation of that amazing game back from when I played on my Amiga!

Control minions to mine and build your underground lair… building training rooms to bring in warriors to your dungeon, libraries to bring in cultists, build farms to keep them happy and fed, beds for them to sleep in… treasury rooms to keep all the gold you mine… blacksmith to build traps and bridges… many more rooms…. And use it all to slay and defeat the do gooders invading your dungeon!!

Can rotate and zoom in to the action, graphics are nice and clean… voice overs and sound effects are perfect!

There’s a map that you have to advance along each with more difficult good guys to defeat and tougher dungeons to defend.

Be the overseer that you have always wanted to be…. Destroy them my minions!!!

HIGHLY RECCOMEND!!

Devs, will you be mailing an Underworld Overseer 2 and 3? :)

This game relaxes your brain with all the simultaneous tasks it gives you.
It is such a pleasure hovering over your dungeon and building and creating. 

The graphics are awesome, I love cell shading a lot. 

The game satisfies but I wish it did the following to level itself up.

Developers: 

I wish the whole game was on the difficultie of the later levels. It’s just too easy (even if it’s fun) and doesn’t present much of a challenge like the later ones do.

I also wish you gave the option of sending different parties to different locations at the same time. Or maybe you did and I didn’t get the difference between raid and rally. 

Kind of like old school Warcraft and Red Alert. 

Regardless I had such a good time with this game. Addictive and relaxing.

If you enjoy real-time strategy, base building, and dungeon keeper style games, you are in for a treat.  I only played for a little over an hour, but it doesn't take long to realize the amount of passion and love the developers put into this game.  
This was made by the same devs that made Deisim, another fun strategy game that never received the praise it deserved.  
This game is fun, and I see myself spending tons of hours in this trying to perfect my hideout and keeping it safe from them pesky humans😆

Do yourself a favor and give this gem a try.  You will be pleasantly surprised!!

I grew up playing the first Dungeon Keeper, but I never really enjoyed its sequel or any of its knock offs through the years.

I pretty much gave up on a truly great spiritual successor, and I only really play VR games now anyway...

So imagine my surprise when this dropped... And it's actually good! I've been playing it non-stop since I got it. Half way through Hard mode right now.

So much fun, so nostalgic, so well designed! I will be following these developers from now on.

P.S: A level editor would take this game to the next level.

Super nice game with cool graphics and mechanics. 
Good soundtrack 

But not enough “rooms” can’t wait to see more! 

Good job to the team!)
Its Dungeon Keeper in vr need I say more? well done devs sterling work👍🏻
I never played dungeon keeper but this game got me hooked for the style very fun easy interaction with everything love it!
This game scratched that itch i was looking for in VR, thank you devs

My review

I completed the whole game except hardcore. This game has so much potential if they update more with new units, spells, enemy’s. All I have to say is great game. This reminds me of a demeos strategy wise. So if you like demeos I suggest you buy this or any other strategy game. 

Issues

But their is some waste potential in the game is only having 2 or 1 spawner for the bone fairs, then after this their all in cages which means if they die they are done for. But the problem is that their very important as you can’t even use spells on bridges which means they are very important to grab bones and bring it to the cemetery, which is a waste of skeletons and cemetery I could have had. Also the fact I have to guess how much food and lairs I need kind of sucks. I just have to keep on placing until he stops saying we need more lairs or farms. Wish added a number thing for lairs and farms.

Game breaking bug??? God please nerf this.

But also another thing that is game breaking the fact that I can put units on unclaim land which means I can claim the land with the miners that is super far away. Even worst is the fact I can cheese bosses and level in 8 min as I did with the missions on the 2nd last boss where you kill the champions so I just attacked the champions and cheese the last level at 18 minutes spamming golems which keep in mind don’t cost me anything and infinitely spawn. Even breaking out of cages is easy with placing a single miner and even putting them in infinite gold stash in the middle of where nothing can reach it.

This is the best RTS I’ve played thus far on the quest.

If you like controlling a bunch of little units, building things that give you perks/abilities, using spells and defending your stuff, you’ll like this game.
Keep up the good work devs! I love this type of game and it's even better in VR. Highly recommended if love dungeon keeper or similar titles.
Game is pretty fun has a lot of depth to it than you can mess around with.

Would like to be able to pick up creatures from above without having to continuously come down to grab.. also would like to see the "possession" spell included as in (dungeon keeper) where you could see everything from the perspective of the creature's eyes you were possessing. good game
Saw this while I was playing Deisim, and decided to check it out. 10/10 App way better then I expected for an early access.

I love dungeon building games. It is a sadly small industry in the gaming world, but I still find it great. With this game, it's finally come to the vr scene. Underworld Overseer is a great game to play. Alongside the classic dungeon building, mechanics is a slight egyptian/Arabic theme. This is an interesting theme to add. You can comfortably move the view while sitting down , throw plenty of spells, and drop minions outside of territory. You don't have to worry about workers going off on their own too much since you can dictate what gets claimed. And there is no level system, making minions easy to replace. However, imps have a habit of not retreating and getting themselves killed, plus the gold doesn't really generate fast enough to really be viable for the long term. There is no sandbox mode or way to fill up a tile with a wall. Plus, it takes too long for minions to train into elites. Overall, it was a great game, but it has little flaws that need improved.
I want more from this game. I absolutely love this game. It needs more to do but it's a beautiful and fun game. The devs did a wonderful job and can't wait to see what else they do in the future.

reviews.review_title: Was a little slow getting into it
reviews.review_description:
Was a little slow getting into it, but now I can't stop.

It's my teenage years, but futuristic!

reviews.review_title: Thoroughly enjoyed it. Could be tweaked
reviews.review_description:
I didn’t want to leave a review until I had completed the game in both normal and hard-core modes. Now that that is done here is what I have to think. 

This is an awesome remake of the classic dungeon keeper that I thoroughly enjoyed. The gameplay and ambiance is spot on, however, there are a few tactics that can be employed which make it almost impossible to lose. A little bit of tweaking by the Dave‘s could significantly increase the challenge.

Like other reviewers have noted Golem use can be OP. But there are a few other tactics that are just as OP and should probably also be addressed. I don’t want to give any spoilers so I will leave it at this: I found that by far the most difficult levels to overcome were in the earlier stages where access to certain features were still locked. I would suggest that the developers consider applying more time pressure in the later stages. Having less time to react and prepare wood nullify many of these overpowered ￼tactics that are available to the player in the game’s current format.
Great game, so much fun thanks for making this game it’s so much fun.
Very good game! Non-stop strategy game and bring the Dungeon Keeper vibe! I love it!
This is what I've needed in VR a great game and some great detail.

If you remember Dungeon Keeper and liked it than this game is for you.
It’s a little difficult to get used to the movement but worth it!

I can't wait to see what the devs make next. The game is incredibly fun. Building up your little army with all these tools at your disposal really gives you this sense of power. I love the thick inked lines art style it really works we'll for VR. I'm on to the 5th level so far.

Underworld Overseer really deserves high praise for how you lose track of time by the enjoyment of defending your little minion's base and its accessibility of how the game works, while still maintaining a good level of strategy. It's a nice mix of being able to relax while playing and slight urgency to protect your little dudes lol.
It’s so much fun and a hidden bonus is that it has a mixed reality option, so yeah, this studio makes fun strategy games
